kpi.totalCustomersTooltip: Everyone who has ever signed up for the platform. This is the full base, including people who registered but never made a transaction.
kpi.atRiskTooltip: Customers who were active at some point but have gone silent — no transactions in the last 90 days or more. These are the ones most likely to leave if not re-engaged soon.
kpi.noActivityTooltip: People who signed up but have never made a single transaction. They created an account and stopped — a missed conversion opportunity.

clusters.title: AI Segmentation Groups
clusters.highValueActiveTooltip: Customers with high recency, frequency, and monetary scores. Top-tier engagers who transact regularly and hold premium products like investments and insurance.
clusters.lowValueDormantTooltip: Shallow, low-economics users — lowest frequency, monetary intensity, and product depth. Often expensive to acquire and slow to start, they never built a habit. The story is not 'churned after being great' but 'never really activated.' Actions skew toward acquisition quality, onboarding, and cheap tests before heavy retention spend.
clusters.atRiskChurnerTooltip: Lapsed legacy customers — long tenure and solid historical transaction depth, but currently dormant. High recency days, low recent activity, and large activation gaps despite often owning multiple products. The lever is reactivation and product re-use, not VIP upsell.

cluster.customerCountTooltip: How many customers fall into this segment.
cluster.pctOfTotalTooltip: How large this segment is relative to all {{total}} registered customers. Useful for understanding which group has the most business impact.
cluster.avgRfmTooltip: A score from 1 to 5 summarising how recently, how often, and how much customers in this segment spend. Higher means more engaged and valuable.
cluster.avgAcqCostTooltip: On average, how much was spent in marketing and promotions to bring each customer in this segment onto the platform.

chart.cohortHeatmapDesc: Each row = acquisition cohort. Columns = months since registration (M0…). Colour = % active that month.
chart.m6retention: M6 Active Rate by Channel over Cohorts
chart.m6retentionDesc: % of customers still active 6 months after first transaction, by acquisition cohort.

aiPanel.title: AI Recommendation
aiPanel.notificationTextTooltip: A short, ready-to-send push notification (~160 chars) personalised for this customer. Copy it and use it directly in your CRM or messaging tool.
aiPanel.idleHint: Click Analyze to generate a personalized recommendation.

fields.riskLevelTooltip: How urgently this customer needs attention. Critical = likely to churn soon; High = strong disengagement signals; Medium = early warning; Low = healthy relationship.
fields.recommendedActionTooltip: The single most impactful step the account manager should take with this customer right now.
fields.suggestedProductTooltip: The product or feature most likely to deepen engagement or address the customer's current situation, based on their profile and what they already own.
fields.messageToneTooltip: The emotional register the account manager should use when contacting this customer — e.g. urgent, empathetic, celebratory.
fields.reasoningTooltip: The AI's explanation for its recommendation, grounded in this customer's actual data — RFM scores, tenure, acquisition channel, and product ownership.

customers.title: Customers
customers.expandHint: Click on a customer to expand their profile and generate a personalized AI recommendation.
customers.disclosure: Customer data shown here is synthetic — randomly sampled from a dataset generated with realistic Brazilian demographics and statistical behavioural models for this portfolio project.
customers.clusterTooltip: Customers were grouped by a K-Means clustering process run on RFM behavioural features (Recency, Frequency, Monetary). Three clusters: High Value Active — top-tier engagers who transact regularly; Low Value Dormant — shallow users who never built a habit; At-Risk Churner — lapsed legacy customers with solid history but currently silent.
customers.rotateHint: For a better experience, rotate your phone to landscape.

kpi.rfmScoreTooltip: Composite behavioral score (1–5) combining recency, frequency, and monetary signals. ≥ 4.0 = strong · 2.5–3.9 = moderate · < 2.5 = at risk.
kpi.tenureTooltip: Months since registration. Longer tenure means deeper relationship history. No universal threshold.
kpi.recencyDaysTooltip: Days since last transaction. Lower is better — a customer active yesterday scores lower than one inactive for 90 days. Percentile is inverted: p95 means more recent than 95% of the population.
kpi.avgTicketTooltip: Average transaction value in BRL. Higher tickets indicate greater spending power. p95 means higher than 95% of the population.
kpi.avgDaysBetweenTxTooltip: Average days between transactions. Lower means more frequent engagement. Percentile is inverted: p95 means more frequent than 95% of the population.
kpi.acquisitionCostTooltip: R$ invested to acquire this customer. Lower is better for unit economics. High cost + low RFM = poor ROI. Percentile is inverted: p95 means lower cost than 95% of the population.
kpi.activityTrendTooltip: Direction of engagement over the analysis window, scaled to [−1, +1]. +1 = strongly growing, 0 = stable, −1 = strongly declining. Percentile ranks this customer's trend vs the full population.

hero.heading: An AI system that turns behavioral segments into a personalized next action for every customer.
hero.subhead1: SynaptiqPay is a Brazilian fintech with 8,000 digital wallet customers. The commercial team had rich transaction data but no clear way to segment the base or decide what to offer each person.
hero.subhead2: We built a machine learning segmentation model that groups customers by behavior, then fed those segments — alongside RFM scores, cohort health, and product ownership signals — into an AI agent that recommends the right product for each customer.
hero.subhead3: We also built a business dashboard so the commercial team can monitor segment health, track KPI evolution, and measure the impact of each intervention over time. The goal is higher ROI: retain at-risk customers, grow engaged ones, and turn insight into revenue.

agentCard.label: AI Recommendation
agentCard.customerLine: Ana Souza · 92 days inactive · wallet only · paid ads
agentCard.notificationText: Exclusive offer just for you — unlock cashback on every purchase today.

cards.label: The Problem
cards.body: The commercial team at an 8,000 customer Brazilian fintech were spending hours each week in spreadsheets. There was no behavioral segmentation and no data-driven direction for how to treat each customer.

cards.label: The Brief
cards.body: Understand the current customer base, identify behavioral clusters, and deliver a system where each cluster drives a distinct action: retention for lapsed users, upsell for high-value actives, reactivation for dormant accounts, and activation for customers who never transacted.

cards.label: The Approach
cards.body: Three analysis phases covering demographics, cohort behavior, and RFM scoring, combined with a machine learning segmentation model and an AI recommendation agent built on top.

cards.label: What Was Delivered
cards.body: A business dashboard for ongoing monitoring and an AI agent that generates a personalized recommendation for each customer based on their profile and behavior history.

methodology.heading: How the engagement was structured

phases.title: Data Foundation
phases.description: We generated a synthetic dataset of 8,000 customers using statistical distributions. The data uses behavioral distributions that replicate real churn, dormancy, and product adoption patterns. A unique dataset, nothing that can be found on Kaggle or other sites.

phases.title: Behavioral Analysis
phases.description: Three analysis phases covered who the customers are, how they behave over time, and how cohorts differ in retention and lifecycle stage. RFM scoring (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) distilled transaction history into comparable signals across all 8,000 customers.

phases.title: Segmentation and Intelligence
phases.description: K-Means clustering (unsupervised machine learning) grouped customers into operational segments based on behavioral signals. A LangGraph agent then takes each customer profile and generates a structured recommendation using a large language model.

methodology.callout: Two layers of AI were applied at different stages. K-Means clustering identifies customer segments without labeled training data. The LLM-based agent produces recommendations grounded in each customer's actual behavior, not generic copy.

tools.name: Faker
tools.why: Generates realistic Brazilian customer data with built-in pt_BR locale support — no real PII needed.

tools.name: Pandas
tools.why: Vectorised transforms for RFM scoring and cohort pivots — fast enough for 8,000 customers without a data warehouse.

tools.name: Supabase / Postgres
tools.why: Managed Postgres with a free tier — zero ops overhead, direct psql COPY for bulk loads.

tools.name: SQLAlchemy
tools.why: Bound-parameter queries as the only SQL interface — eliminates injection risk at the API layer.

tools.name: Scikit-learn
tools.why: K-Means clustering on RFM features produces three interpretable operational segments without label noise.

tools.name: LangGraph
tools.why: Conditional graph routing ties structured customer data to the LLM call — prevents hallucinations on generic prompts.

tools.name: OpenRouter
tools.why: Single API key for free-tier LLMs (Gemini Flash, LLaMA 70B) — no billing commitment during development.

tools.name: FastAPI
tools.why: Auto-generated OpenAPI docs and async support with minimal boilerplate — ideal for a small, typed Python API.

tools.name: React + Vite
tools.why: Sub-second HMR during development and a tiny production bundle — no Create React App overhead.

tools.name: Tailwind + shadcn/ui
tools.why: Unstyled component primitives + utility classes give full design control without a heavy component library.

tools.name: Recharts
tools.why: Composable SVG charts with a React-native API — cohort heatmap and segment breakdown without D3 complexity.

tools.name: Railway
tools.why: One-command deploys from GitHub with automatic HTTPS and zero cold-start delay.

product1.title: Business Dashboard
product1.body: A dashboard that gives the commercial team a real-time view of customer segments, RFM scores, and cohort health. No SQL, no spreadsheets. The data is ready to act on.

product2.title: AI Recommendation Agent
product2.body: A LangGraph agent that receives a customer's behavioral profile and returns a structured recommendation. It produces a risk level, a suggested action, a product to offer, and the reasoning behind it.
product2.bodyDynamic: The prompt adapts to the customer's segment: retention for lapsed users, upsell for high-value actives, reactivation for dormant accounts, and activation for those who never transacted.

phases.title: Intelligence Layer
items: Ask the data anything in plain language — no SQL, no dashboards
Every answer surfaces a ready-to-share chart automatically
Safe by design: the chat layer can read data but never modify it
Handles vague questions by asking a follow-up before running a query

phases.title: BI Expansion
items: Identify which acquisition channels generate the highest long-term value
Flag customers likely to leave before they do, with a predictive model
Map acquisition ROI visually — cost vs. lifetime value by segment and channel
